comments.text: Besides the new reference pointed out by Alfaiz, today there has been a new improvement by Hefty, Horn, King and Pfender: https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.19718 The best bound is now $$R(3, k) \geqslant \bigg(\frac{1}{2} + o(1)\bigg) \frac{k^2}{\log k}.$$ (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
This paper has a Lean formalization linked here: https://www.math.cmu.edu/~wes/trellis.php
[AjKoSz80] is unable to load any reference. (The site has been updated to address this comment.)

A new lower bound of\[ R(3,k) \;\geq\; \left(\tfrac{1}{3} + o(1)\right) \frac{k^{2}}{\log k} \,. \]has been given by Marcelo Campos, Matthew Jenssen, Marcus Michelen and Julian Sahasrabudhe in their paper: A new lower bound for the Ramsey numbers R(3,k), 2025. One can find it here: https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.13371 (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
indeed. furthermore, these authors conjecture that $1/2$ should be the true answer.
